title: Complexity, Profiling, Rendering Budgets, and Responsiveness · Runtime Lens: Complexity Model
summary: Optimization starts from intuition, solver and drawing time are combined, huge mazes freeze the event loop, and average latency hides stalls. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the complexity model increment in representative performance and responsiveness report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive time and space from cells, passages, frontier, item count, algorithm, and representation before measuring.
Define representative tasks, accommodations, observation boundaries, barrier severity, consent, privacy, and closure evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-complexity-model — Derive time and space from cells, passages, frontier, item count, algorithm, and representation before measuring.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-performance-profiling-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-performance-profiling-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_performance_profiling_2(cells: int = 4096, solver_ms: int = 18, render_ms: int = 26, cancellation_ms: int = 41, event_budget_ms: int = 50) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or min(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms, event_budget_ms) < 0:
        return False, cells, "profile-shape"
    if solver_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, solver_ms, "solver-budget"
    if render_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, render_ms, "render-budget"
    if cancellation_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, cancellation_ms, "cancel-budget"
    assert max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms) <= event_budget_ms
    return True, max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms), "responsive-capacity-profile"

Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-complexity-model-obx3ta
# Competency: maze-complexity-model.
# Case 9734: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def evidence_maze_complexity_model_obx3ta(cells: int = 4096, solver_ms: int = 18, render_ms: int = 26, cancellation_ms: int = 41, event_budget_ms: int = 50) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_obx3ta = "obx3ta-at3xbo-9734"
    assert evidence_variant_obx3ta
    evidence_axes_obx3ta = (
        "gridobx3ta",
        "topology9734",
        "searchat3xbo",
        "eventobx3ta9734",
        "access9734obx3ta",
        "recoveryat3xbo9734",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_obx3ta)) == 6
    if cells < 1 or min(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms, event_budget_ms) < 0:
        return False, cells, "profile-shape"
    if solver_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, solver_ms, "solver-budget"
    if render_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, render_ms, "render-budget"
    if cancellation_ms > event_budget_ms:
        return False, cancellation_ms, "cancel-budget"
    assert max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms) <= event_budget_ms
    return True, max(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms), "responsive-capacity-profile"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Derive time and space from cells, passages, frontier, item count, algorithm, and representation before measuring.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Complexity Model model · Theory Complexity Model, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Complexity Model precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Complexity Model model · Theory Complexity Model, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 2299 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate algorithm, render, and cancellation profiles under a representative responsiveness budget; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-complexity-model-obx3ta(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_complexity_model_obx3ta\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cells < 1 or min\(solver_ms, render_ms, cancellation_ms, event_budget_ms\) < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?solver_ms > event_budget_ms)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?render_ms > event_budget_ms)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cancellation_ms > event_budget_ms)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
